title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied, 40, from Reading, sentenced for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts, including orangutan and leopard skulls.
Bied received an eight-month prison term, two years supervised release, and a $75,000 fine for wildlife trafficking.
Parts trafficked included leopard skin and skulls, polar bear skull, and narwhal tusk from 2018 to 2021.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black was convicted of first-degree murder for the 2023 fatal stabbing of 19-year-old Milteer Hendricks in Falmouth.
The stabbing occurred outside an apartment complex on East Falmouth Highway during a premeditated attack involving five people.
Black will be sentenced on Friday, facing a mandatory life sentence without parole for the murder conviction.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police Lt. Anthony DeLucia suspended amid investigation into Sgt. Scott Quigley's alleged DUI and fatal crash.
DeLucia allegedly instructed a DA official to keep Quigley's intoxication during the crash a secret, affecting legal proceedings.
Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted for negligent homicide after a December 2023 crash killed Angelo Schettino; his police certification revoked.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker were sentenced to three to five years for manslaughter of their foster child.
The 10-month-old foster child died in 2020 from treatable conditions the couple overlooked, leading to involuntary manslaughter charges.
The couple's sentences will be staggered due to their four other children, and both are barred from future caregiving roles.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, was sentenced to life without parole for murdering boyfriend Leroy Fowler, 55, with antifreeze in 2022.
Church poisoned Fowler's Powerade with ethylene glycol due to jealousy over his longstanding affair, prosecutors stated.
Video evidence showed Church mocking Fowler in distress; DNA linked her to the contaminated Powerade bottle.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox begin 2026 season with a 1-5 record, worst start since 2019, after losing five consecutive games.
Boston's pitching staff ranks last in MLB for home runs allowed per nine innings with a 5.22 ERA.
Key players like Roman Anthony and Trevor Story are struggling offensively with low batting averages and high strikeouts.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, 35, retired from the NFL after 13 seasons, which included a successful stint with the New England Patriots.
Gilmore won a Super Bowl and was named NFL Defensive Player of the Year in 2019 while with the Patriots.
Drafted in 2012, Gilmore played for six teams and finished with 617 tackles, 149 pass deflections, and 32 interceptions.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle initiated global layoffs this week, affecting multiple countries with an estimated total of 20,000 to 30,000 job cuts.
No Oracle layoffs reported in Massachusetts state filings; company offices located in Burlington, Mass. and Nashua, New Hampshire.
Analysts suggest layoffs are part of Oracle's $2.1 billion restructuring plan, amidst a larger tech industry employment reduction trend.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year contract with the Cincinnati Bengals, his third AFC team in three years.
Dugger played nine games for the Steelers last season, recording 42 tackles, two interceptions, and a sack.
Former Patriots safety Dugger saw diminished playing time before his trade to Pittsburgh, where his performance improved.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes returned from vacation to find his truck stolen from Logan Airport's central parking garage.
The Massachusetts State Police are investigating similarities with a previous truck theft from the same garage.
The stolen vehicle was reportedly driven to New Jersey, similar to an earlier incident, but there were no arrests.
